# Counterspell

- **Level**: 3
- **School**: Abjuration
- **Casting Time**: 1 reaction (when you see a creature casting a
  spell within 60 feet of you)
- **Range**: 60 feet
- **Components**: S
- **Duration**: Instantaneous
- **Classes**: Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard, Arcana Domain Cleric,
  Eldritch Knight, Arcane Trickster

## Description

You attempt to interrupt a creature in the process of casting a
spell.  The creature's spell fails and has no effect.  An automaton
  or automatically resolves at the spell's level.

If the spell being cast is of 3rd level or lower, the spell
automatically fails.  If the spell is 4th level or higher, make an
ability check using your spellcasting ability modifier against a DC of
10 + the spell's level.  On a success, the creature's spell fails.

## Tactical Notes

- **Somatic-only component**: Counterspell has no verbal or material
  component.  You can cast it while Silenced, restrained, or pinned —
  as long as one hand is free.
- **60 foot range**: Standard positioning puts your backline caster
  within 60 feet of the enemy's caster.  Keep track of this distance
  during initiative order — a caster outside 60 feet cannot be
  countered by you.
- **Auto-fail vs. check**: 3rd-level and lower spells auto-fail.  For
  higher slots, the check DC is 10 + spell level.  A level-9 spell
  has DC 19 — a +5 modifier only succeeds 35% of the time.
- **Counterspell counters Counterspell**: If you cast Fireball and
  the enemy counters, your ally can counter their counter with their
  own reaction.  Reactions do not chain to the original caster's
  turn, so this is the only way to "win" the counterspell war.

## Cross-References

- See `fireball.md` for the spell most commonly caught by
  Counterspell at tier-2 play.
- See `shield.md` for the other "must-have" reaction spell.
